relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The crowd presses in to hear the word beside the lake.
Empty boats and washed nets wait at the shore.
Simon’s boat becomes a floating teaching place.
Jesus calls Simon from exhaustion into the deep.
Simon trusts the word beyond the failed night.
Abundance breaks the limits of the nets.
Partnership is needed to hold the overflow.
The catch reveals Simon’s own misalignment before holiness.
Astonishment seizes the whole fishing circle.
Fear is answered with a new calling: catching people alive.
They leave everything and follow.
A man full of skin disease asks if Jesus is willing.
Touch and willingness cleanse immediately.
The healing is sent into priestly testimony.
The word spreads and crowds come to hear and heal.
Jesus withdraws into deserted prayer.
Teachers gather while power to heal is present.
Friends carry a paralyzed person toward Jesus.
Blocked by the crowd, they open the roof and lower need into the middle.
Jesus sees trust and speaks release.
Religious reasoning questions who can release misalignment.
Jesus reads the reasoning of hearts.
Release and rising are held together.
The Son of Humanity shows authority to release on earth.
The man rises immediately and goes home glorifying the Divine.
Astonishment fills the room with holy fear.
Levi is called from the tax booth.
He leaves everything and follows.
Levi’s house becomes a table of outsiders and disciples.
The table provokes grumbling.
Jesus names himself physician for those who know their harm.
His call is for misaligned ones into turning.
Fasting becomes the next comparison.
Bridegroom-presence changes the time.
When the bridegroom is taken, fasting will return.
New fabric cannot simply patch the old.
New wine cannot be forced into old skins.
Fresh wine needs fresh containers.
Attachment to the old resists the taste of the new.
